Off Calendar Mar-03-2016 Text Ruling

Defendant Javad Mirsaidi'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attorney CODY JAFFE, ESQ. of Law Offices of Cody Jaffe, Tel No. 415-524-3268 with and for Plaintiff FEI SU as "specially appearing attorney";
Atty. JOHNNY D. KNADLER, ESQ., Tel No. 310-564-6695 appearing for Defendant Javad Mirsaidi;
Atty. IVAN C. JEN, ESQ., Tel No. 415-504-2706 appearing for Defendants Synergy Project Management, Inc. and Donovan Gilliland.

Court and counsel address Mr. Jaffe's appearance today as a "specially appearing attorney." Court permits Mr. Jaffe to argue today on behalf of Plaintiff based on Mr. Jaffe's representation that he will file a substitution of attorney to subtitute in as Plaintiff's counsel by the close of business on 2/3/16. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Javad Mirsaidi's motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. As to issue 1, plaintiff presented admissible evidence that she told DFEH that she had been sexually harassed and thus there is a triable issue regarding an equitable exception to the requirement that she exhaust her administrative remedies. (Su Declaration Exhibits A, D and E; Holland v. Union Pacific Railroad Co. (2007) 154 Cal. App. 4th 940. 947; see generally Chin, et al, Rutter Group Practice Guide on Employment Litigation at 16:291). As to issue 2, plaintiff presented admissible evidence that her employment was terminated and she was not re-hired because of her gender and thus there is a triable issue whether she was discriminated against on the basis of her gender. (Su Declaration paragraphs 1-56). As to issue 3, plaintiff presented admissible evidence that she was not paid all wages owed to her when due and that Mr. Mirsaidi knew that she hadn't been paid what was owed to her and thus there is a triable issue on plaintiff's eleventh claim. (Su Declaration paragraphs 90-107). All relevancy objections are overruled. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and CCP 437(C)(G). Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

MINI MINUTES:

Atty. IVAN C. JEN, Tel No. (415) 504-2706 appearing for Defendant Synergy Project Management, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF FEI SU'S Motion For Order Granting Plaintiff Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted. Plaintiff shall file her proposed amended complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. "The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified." (Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422.) Defendant has not shown it will be unduly prejudiced by the amendment. The trial date is set for approximately six months from now and discovery is ongoing. Defendant will have sufficient time to file a demurrer or motion for summary judgment and to conduct discovery on the new allegations. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
